concept: Set difference A \ B: elements in A but not in B
question: Let A = {1, 3, 5, 7, 9} and B = {3, 6, 7, 8}. What is A \ B?
solution: 1. List elements of A: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9. 2. List elements of B: 3, 6, 7, 8. 3. Remove from A any element that also appears in B: 3 is in B so remove it; 7 is in B so remove it. 4. Remaining elements of A: 1, 5, 9. 5. Therefore A \ B = {1, 5, 9}.
paraphrase: Start by writing out A: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9. Then write out B: 3, 6, 7, 8. Go through each element of A and cross out any that also belong to B. The number 3 appears in B, so it is crossed out. The number 7 also appears in B, so it is crossed out too. The numbers 1, 5, and 9 do not appear in B, so they stay. The result of A \ B is therefore {1, 5, 9}.
